task_scores.task_title: Phase 1 Backend Foundation — Migration, threshold_service, API routes
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: Partial migration on large tables (sessions >1000) can leave process_type NULL if UPDATE locks or times out before ALTER completes — scoring would fail or use wrong defaults.
task_scores.reasoning: Pre-migration checks (row count, migration_preview.sql, manual UPDATE for >1000 rows) reduce migration risk; cache lock fix prevents invalidate_cache race.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 2 — Wire thresholds into extract_features, score_session, get_session_score
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: If process_type is missing or wrong for a session, scoring uses wrong thresholds (e.g. TIG scored as MIG) — backfill in migration plus default mig in code mitigates.
task_scores.reasoning: Core value path; optional thresholds param keeps backward compat; Step 2.3 returns full active_threshold_spec for micro-feedback.

task_scores.task_title: Phase 3 Admin UI + Phase 4 Micro-Feedback and report callouts
task_scores.ships_wrong_risk: Form NaN bug (parseFloat without ||0) and angle_target_degrees=0 are fixed; micro-feedback gate on sessionData && primaryScore prevents flicker.
task_scores.higher_leverage_alternative: Phase 4 callouts (4.3–4.5) could be deferred — admin UI and micro-feedback are the main visible outcomes.
task_scores.reasoning: Admin page is the main user-facing deliverable; form validation and micro-feedback fixes close prior critique; Step 4.6 session_factory ensures test coverage.

critical_issues: Step 1.2: If sessions >1000 and operator skips manual UPDATE, alembic upgrade may partially apply — migration can succeed on add_column but fail on UPDATE, leaving inconsistent state.
Multi-worker cache: In-memory cache invalidates only in the worker that handled PUT; other workers serve stale thresholds until restart — documented but no MVP mitigation.
minor_issues: Step 4.5: Hardcoded fallback 'MIG spec — Target 45° ±5°' is wrong if admin changes MIG seed — low risk for demo.
Step 4.6: session_factory must use generate_expert_session with 10+ frames for valid score — now documented.
suggestions: Add Phase 1 pre-flight script that runs migration_preview.sql and row-count guard and exits with clear instructions if sessions >1000.
Add a smoke check after migration: SELECT process_type FROM sessions WHERE process_type IS NULL — expect 0 rows.
For test_put_threshold_invalidates_cache, assert session has frames (e.g. len(session.frames) >= 10) to avoid false pass from session without frames.
dependency_order: T1 (1.2 migration) before T1 (1.4 service) — service needs weld_thresholds table. T1 (1.4) before T1 (1.5) — API uses get_thresholds. T1 complete before T2 — T2 needs process_type column and threshold_service. T2 complete before T3 — Admin UI and micro-feedback need GET/PUT API and active_threshold_spec in score response.
premortem_top_risk: Operator runs alembic upgrade on staging with sessions >1000 without manual UPDATE first — migration UPDATE locks table and times out, leaving partial migration state.
goal_alignment_notes: These 3 tasks deliver the goal: admin configures thresholds at /admin/thresholds, scoring and micro-feedback use DB values, reports show spec. Critique fixes (migration pre-flight, cache lock, form NaN, micro-feedback gate, session_factory) close prior gaps. Visible outcome within 48h after Phase 3: admin can edit and save; replay and report show configured spec.
